title: Gait-based biometric data analysis system
abstract: Systems and methods for diagnosing a user's condition based on his gait. A sensor module with multiple sensors is placed inside a user's shoe and biometric data is gathered from the sensors when the user takes a step or walks. The data is used to generate loops as the various sets of data is plotted against each other. The loops obtained from the data are then compared against stored loops previously obtained. Based on the results of the comparison, the user's condition is diagnosed using predetermined indicators of specific health issues. Using the biometric data and in conjunction with data from various databases, it can be determined whether the user has a specific condition, whether a specific condition is worsening, or whether a specific condition is improving.
claims: 1. A system for use in determining at least one change in a user's physiological condition, the system comprising: a sensor module comprising at least one sensor for gathering gait-based biometric data from a user, said gait-based data relating to a continuous gait of said user, a first set of said gait-based data being gathered from said sensor module at a first time and a second set of said gait-based data being gathered at a second time; a database for storing signature data, said signature data being data resulting from an analysis of said first set of gait-based data gathered from said sensor module when said user uses said system, said signature data forming at least one signature characteristic loop when data point pairs from said signature data are plotted; and a processing unit for receiving data from said sensor module, said processing unit being for determining characteristic data from said second set of gait-based data received from said sensor module and for comparing characteristics of said characteristic data with characteristics of said signature data by comparing said at least one signature characteristic loop with at least one characteristic loop formed by plotting data point pairs from said characteristic data,
